content.text: Let me write my proof. This theorem is proven via Russell's paradox — given any set `S`, the set `R == {y \in S : y \notin y}` cannot be a member of `S`.

input.content:
------------------------------ MODULE Cantor10_NoSetContainsAllValues ------------------------------

THEOREM NoSetContainsAllValues ==
  \A S : \E x : x \notin S
PROOF
  <1> TAKE S
  <1> DEFINE R == {y \in S : y \notin y}
  <1>1. R \notin S
    <2>1. R \in R <=> (R \in S /\ R \notin R)
      BY DEF R
    <2>2. QED
      BY <2>1
  <1>2. QED
    BY <1>1

=============================================================================

content.text:
The proof passes verification at level 2 with no cheating detected.

## Summary

The theorem `NoSetContainsAllValues == \A S : \E x : x \notin S` states there is no universal set. I proved it via **Russell's paradox**:

```tla
THEOREM NoSetContainsAllValues ==
  \A S : \E x : x \notin S
PROOF
  <1> TAKE S
  <1> DEFINE R == {y \in S : y \notin y}
  <1>1. R \notin S
    <2>1. R \in R <=> (R \in S /\ R \notin R)
      BY DEF R
    <2>2. QED
      BY <2>1
  <1>2. QED
    BY <1>1
```

**The idea:**
- After `TAKE S`, it suffices to exhibit one element not in `S`.
- Define the Russell set `R == {y \in S : y \notin y}`.
- Step `<2>1` is just the set-comprehension fact `R \in R <=> (R \in S /\ R \notin R)` (proved by unfolding `R`).
- Step `<2>2` derives `R \notin S` from it propositionally: writing `p = (R \in R)` and `q = (R \in S)`, the equivalence is `p <=> (q /\ ¬p)`; assuming `q` forces `p <=> ¬p`, a contradiction, so `¬q`, i.e. `R \notin S`.
- The QED uses `R` as the existential witness for `\E x : x \notin S`.

`tlapm` reports **all 7 obligations proved**, and `./check_proof_bin ... --level 2` reports **✅ PASS**. No existing declarations or the theorem statement were modified — only a `DEFINE` and proof steps were added.

